title: Constructors
description: Master Default, Parameterized, Copy, and Move constructors alongside Member Initializer Lists.
learningObjectives: Use Member Initializer Lists to optimize property setting
Contrast copy constructors with move constructors in resource-heavy classes

sections.title: Object Lifecycle and Initializers
sections.content: Constructors are special member functions called automatically when an object is instantiated. Standardizing on Member Initializer Lists (`MyClass() : Member(Val) {}`) is crucial in C++. Unlike assignments inside constructor brackets (`MyClass() { Member = Val; }`), initializers construct members directly with values, avoiding double-initialization overhead for nested custom classes.

sections.title: Initializer Lists and Copy Constructors
